title: ECL envelope v2 (vendored)
description: Vendored copy of eidolons-ecl/schemas/envelope.v2.json for self-contained Vivi validation. Validates the ECL v2.0 envelope sidecar written next to every emitted Vivi artefact. ECL v2.0 adds the OPTIONAL ise (Intent, Source, Entitlement) trust-hierarchy block (ECL v2.0 §6.5); everything else is unchanged from v1.0. Source-of-truth is the central ECL spec at Rynaro/eidolons-ecl; re-vendor when ECL publishes a new envelope schema version. Performative enum and context_delta are inlined (not $ref'd) per ATLAS v1.5.0 precedent. The sibling `schemas/ecl-envelope.v1.json` is retained (not removed) so Vivi's own tooling can still validate a v1.x sidecar received during the ECL §7.3 compatibility window (through 2027-05-13); this file is strict to `2.0`.

envelope_version.description: ECL spec version this envelope conforms to. ECL v2.0 §1.1.1. This vendored file validates v2.0 only; see schemas/ecl-envelope.v1.json for the v1.x sidecar shape (§7.3 compatibility window).

thread_id.description: UUID grouping all envelopes of one logical mission.

parent_id.description: message_id of the immediate causal predecessor. null only on the first envelope of a thread.

performative.description: ECL v2.0 §2. Closed ten-performative set, unchanged from v1.0. Enum inlined (not $ref'd) per ATLAS v1.5.0 precedent.

edge_origin.description: Where the (from, to) edge is declared. ECL v2.0 §3.3.

objective.description: Single sentence stating the goal of this message.

schema_version.description: SemVer of the per-Eidolon profile schema validating this payload.

path.description: Relative path. MUST NOT begin with '/' or contain '..'.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex digest of the payload bytes at emit time.

size_bytes.description: Payload byte count at emit time.

ise.description: ISE trust-hierarchy block. OPTIONAL at v2.0 — envelopes without it remain conformant. ECL v2.0 §6.5.

shape_hint.description: Free-form hint about the shape of the expected reply.

confidence.description: Sender's self-assessed confidence in the payload.

assumptions.description: Material assumptions the receiver should know.

ts.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp at envelope construction.

model.description: Model identifier (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6'). Free-form to allow vendor strings.

^x_[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$.description: Vendor extension fields. Receivers SHALL ignore unknown x_* fields. ECL v2.0 §1.2.3.

version.description: SemVer of the Eidolon, or the literal 'n/a' for 'human' / 'orchestrator'.

assertion_grade.description: Emitter's claim about how the artefact was produced (ECL v2.0 §6.5.1 gate S-1). 'unverified' = emitted without internal checks; 'self-attested' = emitter ran its own validation; 'validated' = emitter ran spec-mandated gates; 'human-reviewed' = operator signed off.

methodology_version.description: Eidolon methodology + semver, e.g. 'vivi-1.3.0'. ECL v2.0 §6.5.2.

tool_surface.description: Distinct tool primitives invoked during artefact production (e.g. 'Read','Bash').

lateral_consults.description: Inline-recorded sibling-Eidolon consults that informed this artefact.

auto_route.description: Receiver MAY hand the envelope to the next contracted edge without operator confirm.

auto_merge.description: Receiver MAY merge the artefact into a mainline branch without operator confirm.

auto_deploy.description: Receiver MAY deploy/publish without operator confirm.

receiver_authorization.description: ECL v2.0 §6.5.3. Receivers MUST NOT take an action whose corresponding flag is false (gate S-2).
